title: Compact TOML Promote To Plan
goal: Resolve #330 by making promote-to-plan understand compact TOML active_items in .agentic-workspace/planning/state.toml.
Keep the command compatible with legacy Markdown-style TODO rows.
Prove the dogfooding path with focused tests and a real compact-state command run.
non_goals: Do not redesign planning state or active item schema.
Do not close or materially reshape #230.

intent.outcome: Agents can promote compact TOML active items into canonical execplan records with the package command.
intent.constraints: Preserve legacy Markdown compatibility and keep state.toml as the authority.
intent.latitude: Choose the smallest parser/rewrite path that supports compact active_items cleanly.
intent.escalation: Stop before broad planning state migration or branch changes.

execution.next_step: Patch promote-to-plan to read and rewrite compact TOML active_items, then add regression tests.
execution.proof: Focused planning tests, ruff, package command dogfooding against compact TOML, summary/checker, and diff check.

invariants: Compact TOML state remains canonical for active planning state.
Legacy Markdown TODO promotion remains supported.
The active item points at the canonical .plan.json execplan after promotion.

intent_continuity.larger intended outcome: make package planning helpers dogfoodable against the repo-owned compact planning state

iterative_follow_through.what this slice enabled: promote-to-plan now finds compact TOML active_items and queued_items, writes the matched row back structurally, and points compact rows at the canonical .plan.json record.
iterative_follow_through.intentionally deferred: broader planning schema redesign and deletion of #324-retained roadmap lanes
iterative_follow_through.discovered implications: Dogfooding exposed that BOM-prefixed TOML could fall through to legacy parsing on Windows-authored files, so the TOML reader now tolerates a UTF-8 BOM.

intent_interpretation.inferred intended outcome: continue the remaining executable planned lane with package dogfooding, proof, commit, push, and issue closure
intent_interpretation.chosen concrete what: resolve #330, the remaining concrete open friction issue besides broad #230
intent_interpretation.review guidance: reject fixes that only document the limitation instead of supporting compact TOML active_items

delegated_judgment.requested outcome: ship compact TOML support for promote-to-plan
delegated_judgment.hard constraints: stay on master; keep #230 open; preserve legacy Markdown behavior
delegated_judgment.agent may decide locally: exact helper shape and regression coverage
delegated_judgment.escalate when: support requires broad planning schema redesign

execution_bounds.allowed paths: planning package source/tests plus active planning state and execplan closeout
execution_bounds.required validation commands: uv run pytest packages/planning/tests/test_installer.py -q -k "promote_todo_item_to_execplan"; uv run ruff check packages/planning/src/repo_planning_bootstrap/installer.py packages/planning/tests/test_installer.py; uv run agentic-planning-bootstrap promote-to-plan <compact-item> --target <tmp> --format json; uv run agentic-workspace summary --format json; uv run python scripts/check/check_planning_surfaces.py; git diff --check
execution_bounds.ask-before-refactor threshold: stop before broad planning lifecycle redesign
execution_bounds.stop before touching: unrelated roadmap lanes or branch state

context_budget.live working set: #330, promote-to-plan implementation/tests, compact state.toml shape, active execplan
context_budget.recoverable later: full planning history and unrelated roadmap lanes
context_budget.externalize before shift: update this execplan execution_run, proof_report, and closure fields
context_budget.pre-work config pull: uv run agentic-workspace preflight and summary; gh issue view #330
context_budget.tiny resumability note: support compact TOML active_items in promote-to-plan
context_budget.context-shift triggers: promotion support expands into planning schema redesign

stop_conditions.stop when: #330 is implemented, validated, dogfooded, committed, pushed, archived, and closed
stop_conditions.escalate on scope drift: work starts changing unrelated roadmap or memory behavior
stop_conditions.escalate on proof failure: compact-state promotion cannot be validated with package command

active_milestone.scope: promote-to-plan compact TOML active item support

immediate_next_action: Patch promote-to-plan to load compact TOML active_items before falling back to legacy Markdown rows.
Add regression coverage for compact TOML promotion and existing execplan refusal.
invariants: Legacy Markdown TODO rows remain supported.
Promotion does not leave direct-task fields in active_items.
completion_criteria: promote-to-plan finds compact TOML active_items by id.
Promotion writes the active item surface to the execplan reference and removes direct next/done fields.
Existing execplan references in compact TOML still refuse manual review.
Legacy Markdown TODO promotion tests still pass.
The fixed command is dogfooded against compact TOML state.

execution_run.what happened: Added compact TOML active_items/queued_items support to promote-to-plan, preserved the legacy Markdown fallback, pointed compact items at canonical .plan.json records, and made the TOML state reader tolerate UTF-8 BOMs discovered during CLI dogfooding.
execution_run.scope touched: Planning package promote implementation, planning package tests, active planning state, and execplan closeout.
execution_run.validations run: uv run pytest packages/planning/tests/test_installer.py -q -k "promote_todo_item_to_execplan"; uv run ruff check packages/planning/src/repo_planning_bootstrap/installer.py packages/planning/tests/test_installer.py; uv run agentic-planning-bootstrap promote-to-plan dogfood-compact --target <tmp> --format json; uv run agentic-planning-bootstrap promote-to-plan dogfood-bom --target <tmp> --format json
execution_run.result for continuation: #330 is complete; broader roadmap lane remains visible per #324 and should wait for fresh friction.
execution_run.next step: Run summary/checker/diff validation, archive this plan, close #330, commit, and push.

finished_run_review.scope respected: yes; implementation stayed in planning package promote behavior/tests and planning closeout surfaces
finished_run_review.proof status: proved by focused tests, ruff, and direct CLI dogfooding against compact TOML state including a BOM-prefixed file
finished_run_review.intent served: yes; package promotion no longer requires legacy Markdown TODO rows for compact state
finished_run_review.config compliance: yes; work ran from checked-in active planning state and dogfooded the package command
finished_run_review.misinterpretation risk: low; #230 remains open and #324-retained lanes were not deleted

proof_report.validation proof: 5 focused promote tests passed; ruff passed; repaired CLI created canonical .plan.json records and updated compact state rows in normal UTF-8 and BOM-prefixed TOML dogfood runs.
proof_report.evidence for \"proof achieved\" state: promote-to-plan actions reported created execplan records and updated compact state surfaces to .agentic-workspace/planning/execplans/<slug>.plan.json.

intent_satisfaction.original intent: Make promote-to-plan support compact TOML active items or clearly route to a supported compact-state path.
intent_satisfaction.evidence of intent satisfaction: The command now reads compact TOML active_items/queued_items, refuses already-routed compact execplan surfaces, updates compact rows structurally, and has regression coverage plus CLI dogfood proof.

system_intent_alignment.relevant system intent: make repo-owned workflow surfaces cheaper for agents to use and verify
system_intent_alignment.slice shaping bias: Prefer package command support over manual scaffolding guidance.
system_intent_alignment.broader-lane validation question: Can agents promote compact active work through the package instead of falling back to hand-authored plan references?

closure_check.why this decision is honest: #330 asked for compact TOML promote-to-plan support or clear routing; the package command now supports compact state directly and is validated through tests and dogfooding.
closure_check.evidence carried forward: Archived execplan, focused tests, CLI dogfood output, and package source/tests.
closure_check.reopen trigger: promote-to-plan again fails to promote a valid compact TOML active_items or queued_items row.

execution_summary.outcome delivered: promote-to-plan supports compact TOML active_items/queued_items and updates compact rows to canonical .plan.json surfaces.
execution_summary.validation confirmed: Focused promote tests, ruff, and package CLI dogfood passed.
execution_summary.follow-on routed to: none for #330; #230 remains the only live open issue and should wait for a fresh product-compression signal.
execution_summary.post-work posterity capture: Archived execplan will carry proof; regression tests document the compact and BOM behavior.
execution_summary.knowledge promoted (Memory/Docs/Config): Planning package tests encode the discovered compact-state and BOM friction.

drift_log: 2026-04-26: Promoted #330 as the next executable lane after #331; #230 remains open as a broad product-compression lane.
2026-04-26: Dogfooding exposed BOM-prefixed TOML fallback behavior; fixed in the same parser path and covered by regression.
